highlights: macOS File Provider support is now built into the desktop app, giving protected folders a more native Finder-facing mount experience.
File Provider startup and restore flows are more resilient, with stronger runtime checks, persisted registration validation, and better lifecycle recovery.
important_changes: Desktop login and session restore now reconcile persisted macOS File Provider roots, restarting stale bridges or unregistering unsafe stale domains when needed.
The File Provider bridge now supports persistent provider state, stable item identifiers, directory-scoped enumeration, and explicit domain signaling for Finder updates.
Provider control and readiness checks are more complete, with register, unregister, signal, and status flows backed by clearer runtime diagnostics.
fixes: Fixed cases where protected folders on macOS did not mount or restore cleanly after app startup, login, or session recovery.
Removed a redundant mounted-folder action in the enrolled folders view, making the macOS folder controls clearer.

highlights: Auto-mount can now restore the last mounted protected folder after login, with a Settings toggle for users who prefer manual mounting.
Public source verification is clearer, with reproducible unsigned macOS build guidance and published hashes for comparing source-built artifacts.
important_changes: The desktop app now remembers the last mounted root safely in local storage and skips auto-mount when the folder is no longer enrolled or already mounted.
Update install UI is split into dedicated frontend methods, with clearer update badges, progress states, restart prompts, and manual update checks in Settings.
Restricted personal builds now explain that team and group administration commands are disabled instead of describing the whole client as individual-only.
fixes: Removed deprecated desktop shell-open and xterm package dependencies, reducing packaging noise and warning output.
Cleaned up Tauri command organization, CLI path handling, notification paths, and release-note loading fallback behavior for public desktop builds.

highlights: Coverage Center is now built into the desktop app so you can review folder coverage, scan progress, and attention items in one place.
Post-quantum protection status is clearer across the workspace home, folder detail view, and header badge.
important_changes: Protected-folder enrollment errors now classify common failures such as permissions, missing paths, and already-protected folders.
Mount progress and timeout messaging now identify the target folder and explain when backgrounding is available.
Force unmount now requires a typed FORCE confirmation before the destructive path can continue.
fixes: Refined empty states, action labels, and workspace copy around coverage and folder protection.
Improved mount and unmount safety guidance for conflict, recovery, and long-running operations.
